phase_name: session 18 complete — Li asymptotics and decomposition validated

completed_tasks.name: SESSION 17: Li coefficients lambda_n via FFT-Taylor for n=1..200
SESSION 17: Gamma/zeta decomposition — zeta oscillates, goes negative n=156-186
SESSION 17: M-function connection (averaging smooths local negativity)
SESSION 18: Asymptotic bound — |lambda_n^zeta| << sqrt(n*log n), GUE suppression
SESSION 18: N_0 = 11 — Gamma dominates for ALL n >= 11 (verified to n=200)
SESSION 18: Avenue 4 DEAD END — truncated Euler product fails in critical strip
SESSION 18: Bombieri-Lagarias — our decomposition superior, tau_n monotone increasing

remaining_tasks.name: SESSION 19: Prove lambda_n^Gamma > 0 for n >= 8 via Stirling analysis
SESSION 19: Rigorous bound on lambda_n^zeta (Stieltjes constant route?)
Extend to n=500 with 500dp precision (confirm oscillation pattern)

decisions.decision: N_0 = 11 is the Gamma dominance threshold
decisions.rationale: For all n >= 11, lambda_n^Gamma > |lambda_n^zeta| (verified to n=200). At n=11: Gamma=1.50, |zeta|=1.25. Gap widens rapidly.

decisions.decision: Truncated Euler product (Avenue 4) is a dead end
decisions.rationale: Euler product doesn't converge for Re(s) < 1. FFT circle includes Re(s)=0.6 points. Li coefficients from truncated product DIVERGE. d_1 goes to -inf instead of gamma.

decisions.decision: Our smooth_gamma + log_sz decomposition is superior to Bombieri-Lagarias
decisions.rationale: B-L trivial zero sum S_0(n) diverges to -10^17 at n=100. Our split keeps Gamma ~ n*log(n) and zeta ~ O(1). More numerically stable and conceptually cleaner.
Consistent with Keiper-Li conjecture (stronger than RH). Every tau_{n+1} > tau_n for n=1..99.

next_action: Session 19: (1) Prove lambda_n^Gamma > 0 for n >= 8 analytically (Stirling expansion of the smooth_gamma piece), (2) Attempt to bound lambda_n^zeta using Stieltjes constant estimates, (3) Avenue 5: Connes prolate operator — fresh approach entirely. If (1) and (2) succeed, RH reduces to finite verification of n=1..10.
context_notes: Sessions 17-18 built a complete computational framework for Li's criterion. Key scripts: li_criterion.py, li_prime_anatomy.py, li_extended.py, li_highorder.py (200dp), li_M_connection.py, li_asymptotics.py, li_euler_product.py, li_bombieri_lagarias.py. The decomposition lambda_n = lambda_n^Gamma + lambda_n^zeta with Gamma = smooth_gamma(s) and zeta = log((s-1)*zeta(s)) is the central tool. FFT-based Cauchy integral for Taylor coefficients (mpmath.taylor fails). Environment: /c/Python313/python.exe, data: _zeros_500.npy.
